meta_title: Window Cleaning in Winter Salmon Arm — Worth It?
meta_desc: Is window cleaning worth it in Salmon Arm winters? Honest answer for homeowners and businesses, plus why a fall clean beats a winter one every time.
h1: Window Cleaning in Winter Salmon Arm: What Actually Makes Sense
intro: Every winter in Salmon Arm, we get asked the same question: can you clean windows when it's cold out? The honest answer is nuanced — sometimes yes, sometimes no, and sometimes the better move is to wait. Here's the real breakdown so you can make the right call for your home or business.

sections.h2: The Honest Answer: Winter Cleaning Depends on the Job
sections.body:
Window cleaning in sub-zero temperatures isn't impossible, but it's genuinely harder and the results are less reliable. The core problem is physics: water-based cleaning solutions freeze before the squeegee can remove them, leaving ice streaks that are worse than the dirt you started with. Professional-grade antifreeze additives (methanol-based products) push the safe working temperature down to around -10°C, but that's a ceiling, not a guarantee. Below that threshold, even treated solutions start failing.

For residential homes in Salmon Arm, most of the exterior window cleaning season winds down in late October or November. Once overnight lows are consistently near or below zero, the risk of freezing mid-clean — or of a window surface being too cold to release water — makes exterior cleaning unreliable. Homeowners who push for a December clean often end up with streaky glass that has to be redone in spring anyway.

That said, there are real exceptions. Interior-only window cleaning works year-round regardless of outside temperature. If you have a home office with grimy interior glass, or you want the inside of your sunroom windows cleaned before the holidays, a winter interior clean is perfectly sensible. The temperature constraint is purely an exterior problem.
bullets: Solutions can freeze on contact with cold glass below -5°C
Squeegee rubber stiffens in cold and loses its edge-seal effectiveness
Interior-only cleaning is unaffected by outside temperature
Professional antifreeze additives extend safe working range to approximately -10°C

sections.h2: When Winter Cleaning Makes Sense for Commercial Properties
sections.body:
Commercial properties in Salmon Arm often have a stronger case for winter window cleaning than residential homes. The calculation is different when dirty windows mean lost revenue. A restaurant on the main strip with fogged-up winter condensation and fingerprints on the door glass every morning has an ongoing image problem — and waiting until April to address it isn't viable.

For commercial clients, we evaluate winter jobs case by case. Heated interior vestibules, enclosed storefronts, or spaces where cleaning can happen during business hours (when interior heat warms the glass) are workable. Flat commercial windows with no sills for water to pool and refreeze are easier than complex residential window profiles. If the forecast shows a few days above 2°C, a commercial clean can be scheduled strategically.

Property managers who maintain multi-unit residential or commercial buildings sometimes schedule winter cleans for common-area interior glass — lobbies, stairwells, enclosed corridors — where the environment is fully controlled. These jobs proceed identically to a summer clean.

The businesses that benefit most from staying on a year-round schedule are those in the Shuswap's tourism and hospitality sector: hotels near Shuswap Lake, restaurants downtown, and retail shops that stay open for the ski and snowmobile crowd. If your business attracts visitors even in winter, your windows still need to represent you.
bullets: Restaurants and cafes: entry glass and interior windows are cleanable year-round
Hotels and lodges: lobbies and interior-facing glass not dependent on exterior temperature
Retail storefronts: can be scheduled during mild spells above 2°C
Multi-unit buildings: common-area interior glass is unaffected by season

sections.h2: What Temperature Does to Window Cleaning Equipment
sections.body:
Understanding why cold is a problem helps homeowners and business owners set realistic expectations. It's not just about the cleaning solution — cold affects the entire cleaning process from start to finish.

Squeegee rubber is the most temperature-sensitive component. At normal working temperatures, a good rubber blade is pliable and conforms perfectly to the glass surface, pulling water cleanly in a single pass. Below about 5°C, rubber hardens and loses its flex. The blade can't hug the glass evenly, and it leaves water trails that dry into streaks. Experienced cleaners warm their rubber before starting, but this is only a partial fix in sustained cold.

Cleaning solution behavior changes too. Even with antifreeze additives, very cold glass can cause solution to thicken and become difficult to spread evenly. If the glass temperature itself is below zero — which happens on north-facing windows after a cold night — the solution can freeze on contact before you've applied it to the whole pane. You end up chasing ice around the glass.

Water spots also form faster in cold. When solution dries before you can squeegee it, it leaves behind dissolved mineral deposits. Salmon Arm's water is moderately hard, which means there are enough dissolved minerals in untreated tap water to leave visible spots if drying outpaces cleaning. In warm weather this is manageable. In cold, it's a constant battle.
sections.body2: Finally, there's the physical safety element. Wet ladders, icy paths, and cold fingers all increase risk for the cleaner. Professional window cleaning companies factor safety into their winter decisions — not just results.

sections.h2: What Homeowners Should Do Instead: Preparing for Spring
sections.body:
If winter exterior cleaning isn't the right move for most Salmon Arm homes, what should homeowners actually do between November and March? The answer is: use the winter to set yourself up for a great spring clean.

First, check your window seals and frames while windows are closed up tight. Condensation forming between double-pane glass indicates a failed seal — a problem no amount of cleaning will fix. Better to identify this in winter and get a replacement quote, rather than discover it when you call for your spring clean.

Second, address interior glass that accumulated grime through fall. Cooking residue, pet nose-prints, and dust buildup on interior surfaces can be cleaned anytime. A DIY interior clean in January — or a professional interior-only visit — means your spring appointment covers only the exterior.

Third, plan your gutters for spring. Salmon Arm's fall leaf drop loads gutters heavily through October and November. By February, those leaves have composted into a dense sludge. Scheduling a combined window and gutter clean in early April — before the spring rains — is the single highest-leverage maintenance move for most homes. Book it in February before the calendar fills.

Finally, if you didn't get a fall clean before the freeze, don't panic. Winter grime — road salt mist, condensation residue, frost etching — is cleanable in spring with standard methods. It's not a permanent problem.
bullets: Check seals in winter — failed IGUs are a repair job, not a cleaning job
Clean interior glass any time of year without restriction
Book your spring clean (windows + gutters) in February before prime slots go
Winter grime from frost and salt cleans normally in spring — no panic needed

sections.h2: Why a Fall Clean Before the Freeze Is the Right Move
sections.body:
The single best thing a Salmon Arm homeowner can do is schedule a professional window clean in late September or October, before the first hard freeze. This is the move that avoids the winter dilemma entirely.

A good fall clean removes summer's accumulated pollen, wildfire smoke particulate (a real factor in the Shuswap), and insect residue before it has a chance to bake onto the glass through a winter of freeze-thaw cycles. Glass that goes into winter dirty comes out of winter with bonded mineral deposits and etched surfaces that are much harder to clean in spring. Glass that goes in clean just needs a quick spring freshen-up.

For gutters, the timing argument is even stronger. Cleaning gutters in late October — after the leaves have mostly fallen but before the ground freezes — means water flows freely all winter and you avoid the ice dam risk that comes with blocked gutters in a freeze-thaw climate like Salmon Arm's.

Wagon Windows typically books out the October window cleaning rush quickly. If you want a fall clean, calling in September puts you in the best position. Don't wait until November when availability gets tight and the weather is already borderline.
bullets: Fall clean removes wildfire smoke and pollen before it bonds to glass over winter
Clean windows entering winter = much easier spring maintenance clean
Late October gutter cleaning prevents ice dam risk through freeze-thaw cycles
September booking = best availability for October fall clean slots

faqs.q: Can Wagon Windows clean my windows in December in Salmon Arm?
faqs.a: Interior-only cleaning, yes — any time of year. Exterior cleaning in December depends on temperature. If we have a mild spell above 2°C and the forecast holds, we can schedule it. But we won't guarantee streak-free results when glass temperatures are near or below freezing, and we'll tell you honestly if we think you're better off waiting until spring.

faqs.q: What temperature is too cold for window cleaning?
faqs.a: Below -5°C exterior temperature, standard window cleaning solutions become unreliable. With antifreeze additives, we can work down to around -10°C in suitable conditions. Glass surface temperature matters as much as air temperature — north-facing windows on a cold morning can be below zero even when the air is a few degrees above.

faqs.q: My windows look terrible after winter. Is that permanent?
faqs.a: No. Winter grime — road spray, frost residue, condensation minerals — is normal and fully cleanable in spring with professional methods. If you see haze that won't clear after cleaning, that's likely a failed window seal (moisture between panes), which is a repair issue, not a cleaning issue.

faqs.q: When should I book my spring window clean in Salmon Arm?
faqs.a: February or early March for the best April availability. Spring cleaning season — when the weather reliably allows exterior work — typically opens in late March to early April in the Shuswap. The calendar fills fast. Booking two months out ensures you get a date that works before the prime May slots are gone.

meta_title: Post-Construction Window Cleaning Shuswap | New Build Specialist
meta_desc: New build windows need specialist cleaning to remove construction dust, paint overspray, and adhesive residue without scratching. What Shuswap homeowners need to know.
h1: Post-Construction Window Cleaning in the Shuswap: What New Build Windows Actually Need
intro: Moving into a new home in Salmon Arm or Armstrong is exciting — but the windows that look fine from a distance often tell a different story up close. Construction dust, paint overspray, sticker adhesive, and silicone residue are nearly universal on new build windows, and they require a very different approach than routine cleaning. Getting this first clean right sets the baseline for every clean that follows.

sections.h2: What's Actually on New Build Windows (It's Not Just Dust)
sections.body:
Post-construction contamination on windows is a mix of several distinct substances, each with different chemistry and removal requirements. Understanding what you're dealing with explains why a standard window cleaning isn't the right tool for the job.

Construction dust is the most visible issue, but it's also the most dangerous for glass. Drywall dust, concrete dust, and silica from cutting operations are all abrasive. The key risk isn't the dust itself — it's what happens when abrasive dust gets trapped under a squeegee or scrubber and is dragged across the glass surface. This is how new windows get scratched during improper post-construction cleaning. The pre-rinse step that professional cleaners use isn't ceremonial — it's the most important scratch-prevention measure on a new build job.

Paint overspray is the second major contaminant. Trade painters rarely mask every inch of glass, and spray drift reaches surprisingly far. On most new builds, there will be fine paint dots, splatter patterns, or edge runs on at least some windows. Water-based latex paint overspray, if caught fresh, rinses off. Older paint or oil-based products need controlled scraping with a fresh razor blade held at the correct angle. Done wrong, this scratches glass. Done right, it removes paint cleanly without a mark.

Label adhesive from manufacturer stickers is nearly universal on new windows. Some builders leave these on for protection during construction; others don't bother removing them before handover. The adhesive under labels is designed for permanence, and soap and water don't touch it. Proper adhesive removal requires a solvent step — most professionals use an orange-oil based product — before the standard clean.
bullets: Construction dust: abrasive, must be pre-rinsed before any scrubbing to prevent scratches
Paint overspray: requires controlled razor blade removal with proper technique
Label adhesive: solvent-based removal required before water-based cleaning
Silicone caulk residue: leftover from sealing frames, requires specific solvents
Grout haze: present on windows near tile work, needs acid-based or mechanical removal

sections.h2: Why New Build Windows Are Scratch-Risk Situations
sections.body:
New build windows occupy a risk category that experienced window cleaners treat differently from standard residential maintenance cleans. The scratch risk is genuinely elevated, for reasons that aren't always obvious.

Tempered glass — standard on most patio doors, side lites, and lower windows in modern construction — has a phenomenon called 'fabricating debris' or 'glass scratching syndrome.' During the manufacturing process, tiny glass particles can become embedded in the surface of tempered glass. These particles aren't visible to the naked eye, but when a scrubber or squeegee passes over them, they act like embedded sandpaper and create fine scratches. This is not a cleaning error — it's a manufacturing quality issue — but the scratches show up during the first clean, and untangling responsibility requires understanding this phenomenon.

The practical implication: professional post-construction cleaners should inspect tempered glass carefully, use soft scrubbers rather than steel wool or abrasive pads, and document the condition of each pane before starting. If fabricating debris is present, mechanical scrubbing on that glass creates scratches that aren't the cleaner's fault but are the cleaner's problem to manage.

Beyond fabricating debris, the combination of construction grit and new glass simply demands caution. A window that's been through a building site has been exposed to conditions that standard window cleaning technique doesn't account for. Pre-rinsing with low pressure to float off loose abrasive, then applying solution, then cleaning — rather than the standard scrub-immediately approach — is the right sequence for new builds.
sections.body2: Ask any professional you hire whether they've done post-construction cleans before and what their scratch-prevention protocol is. A vague answer is a red flag.

sections.h2: Salmon Arm and Armstrong New Build Context
sections.body:
The Shuswap region — particularly Salmon Arm and Armstrong — has seen sustained residential construction growth over the past several years. Housing demand from people relocating from the Lower Mainland and Alberta has driven new development in areas like Grandview Bench, North Broadview, and expanding subdivisions on the hillside above downtown Salmon Arm. Armstrong's agricultural land base and lower lot costs have made it a target for new residential projects as well.

This construction activity means post-construction window cleaning is not a rare specialty job in this region — it's a regular part of the seasonal workload. New build homeowners in the Shuswap face a few factors that make their situation specific:

Wildfire smoke season (typically July through September) can deposit a fine particulate film on windows during construction if the build spans a summer. This adds an additional layer to the post-construction contamination that isn't always present in other regions.

Hard water from municipal systems (and especially from well water on rural properties near Salmon Arm) means that any residual moisture on new windows during construction can leave mineral deposits on glass that's already contaminated with construction dust. These deposits mineralize quickly and require acid-based treatment to remove.

New builds that back onto agricultural land — common in the Armstrong area — face seasonal dust exposure from field operations: tilling, harvesting, and irrigation system spray all deposit material on windows during construction.
bullets: Grandview Bench and North Broadview among active residential development areas in Salmon Arm
Armstrong: growing new residential activity driven by land availability and lower lot costs
Wildfire smoke particulate is a regional factor for builds completed through summer
Hard water and agricultural dust compound post-construction contamination in this area

sections.h2: What to Ask Your Builder About Window Cleaning Responsibility
sections.body:
One of the most common sources of confusion for new build homeowners is who pays for the post-construction window clean. The answer depends entirely on what's in your purchase contract or builder's warranty, and it's worth clarifying before you close.

Many production builders include a 'construction clean' in their delivery package, but this is typically performed by a general cleaning crew, not window specialists. A construction clean usually means vacuuming, wiping surfaces, and mopping — not the specialist post-construction window cleaning we're describing here. If you ask your builder what their window cleaning process is and the answer is 'our cleaning crew handles it,' it's worth asking specifically whether they remove paint overspray, adhesive residue, and construction dust from glass with appropriate specialty products.

Some premium builders include a proper specialist post-construction window clean in the delivery package. If yours does, confirm that the company they use has post-construction experience — not just standard residential experience.

If the builder's clean doesn't include specialist window care, budget for it separately. On a typical Shuswap new build with 20-35 windows, a proper post-construction clean typically runs more than a standard residential maintenance clean, reflecting the additional time, specialized products, and higher skill level required. It's a one-time cost that's worth paying correctly rather than cutting corners on.

Get clarity on this in writing before the handover walkthrough. Walking through a new home and noting window condition in the deficiency list — if the builder's clean hasn't addressed the glass adequately — is your documentation for getting it resolved under warranty.
bullets: Ask specifically whether the builder's clean includes paint overspray and adhesive removal
General construction cleaning ≠ specialist post-construction window cleaning
Document window condition in the handover deficiency list if standard of clean is inadequate
Budget post-construction specialist cleaning as a separate line if builder doesn't include it

sections.h2: The First Clean Sets the Baseline for Everything After
sections.body:
Here's why the post-construction clean deserves more attention and budget than any subsequent routine clean: it establishes the starting point for the entire life of those windows.

If paint overspray isn't fully removed and gets left to harden, it becomes progressively harder to clean over time. The same is true for silicone residue and adhesive. These substances bond more strongly to glass with every freeze-thaw cycle and season of UV exposure. A post-construction clean done six months after move-in is significantly harder than one done at handover.

More importantly, any contamination that remains on glass after the first clean gets 'overcoated' by subsequent cleaning. Regular window cleaning — standard squeegee and solution work — doesn't penetrate bonded paint or silicone residue. Each subsequent clean makes the glass look cleaner in the moment, but the underlying contamination is still there, and eventually it becomes visible as a persistent haze or patch that won't clean out.

For homeowners investing in a new Shuswap build, the post-construction clean is the one appointment that protects the quality of the glass for the life of the home. Do it right, do it soon after handover, and do it with someone who knows the difference between a post-construction clean and a standard residential visit.

Wagon Windows handles post-construction window cleaning in Salmon Arm, Armstrong, and throughout the Shuswap. We assess each job before starting, document pane condition, and use the right product sequence for each contaminant type. Call David at (403) 837-5092 to discuss your new build.
bullets: Bonded paint and silicone harden over time and become progressively harder to remove
Standard routine cleaning does not penetrate bonded post-construction residue
Best timing: as soon as possible after builder handover, before first winter
A proper first clean protects glass quality for the life of the home

faqs.q: How is post-construction window cleaning different from regular window cleaning?
faqs.a: Post-construction cleaning addresses contaminants that standard window cleaning doesn't touch: paint overspray, label adhesive, silicone caulk residue, and abrasive construction dust. It requires solvent pre-treatment for adhesives, controlled razor blade work for paint, and a careful pre-rinse protocol to avoid dragging abrasive grit across glass surfaces. It typically takes longer and costs more than a routine maintenance clean.

faqs.q: Can I clean new build windows myself to save money?
faqs.a: For basic dust removal on easily accessible windows, yes. But paint overspray and adhesive removal involve chemistry and technique that's easy to get wrong. Improper razor blade use on tempered glass and the wrong solvent on certain window films or coatings can cause permanent damage. For anything beyond light dust, a specialist is worth the cost — especially given the investment in a new home.

faqs.q: My builder says the windows were cleaned. Why do they still look dirty?
faqs.a: Builder delivery cleans are usually general construction cleans, not specialist window cleaning. The crew wipes down surfaces but typically doesn't remove paint overspray, adhesive residue, or do the specialist pre-rinse work required for construction-contaminated glass. This is common across the industry. Request a specialist post-construction clean as a deficiency item, or budget for it independently.

faqs.q: How soon after moving in should I get a post-construction window clean?
faqs.a: As soon as possible after handover — ideally within the first month. Paint overspray and adhesive residue are easier to remove fresh. Waiting through a winter allows freeze-thaw cycles to bond contaminants more firmly to the glass. If your build was completed in fall, aim to get the clean done before the first hard freeze.

h1: Storefront Window Cleaning Benefits: The Business Case for Clean Glass in the Shuswap
intro: A storefront window is a billboard you already own. When it's clean, it sells. When it's dirty, it actively works against you — and research confirms that customers notice, even if they never say it out loud. Here's the full business case for keeping your Salmon Arm storefront windows clean, with numbers that actually make sense.

sections.h2: What Customer Perception Research Actually Shows
sections.body:
The relationship between storefront cleanliness and customer behavior has been studied in retail psychology, and the findings are consistent: dirty windows reduce purchase intent, dwell time, and the likelihood that a new customer will enter at all.

A widely-cited study by the British window cleaning industry — using customer surveys and controlled retail comparisons — found that approximately 52% of shoppers said dirty windows put them off entering a store, and 95% said the cleanliness of a shop window affects their perception of the business's quality. These numbers align with broader research on retail environmental cues: customers use visible signals to infer invisible qualities. A dirty window signals neglect, and neglect in the front of house raises questions about the back.

In practical terms for a Salmon Arm business, this means a significant share of passing foot traffic — potential customers you've already spent money to attract through marketing, signage, and location — is making a subconscious judgment against you based on window condition. You don't get to argue the case. The glass either reinforces or undermines the impression you're trying to make.

For the Shuswap's tourism economy, the stakes are higher. Visitors from the Lower Mainland and Alberta who stop in Salmon Arm are making split-second decisions about where to eat, shop, and spend time. They have no prior relationship with your business and no reason to give you the benefit of the doubt. What they can see from the sidewalk is all they have to go on.

The cost implication of dirty windows isn't a line item you'll find on a P&L, but it's real. If one lost customer per month converts to a $60 average ticket, that's a meaningful number compared to the cost of monthly professional cleaning — which for many Salmon Arm storefronts starts at $169.
bullets: 52% of shoppers say dirty windows deter them from entering a store
95% of customers say window cleanliness affects their quality perception

sections.h2: Light Transmission, Interior Ambiance, and Employee Morale
sections.body:
Clean windows don't just look better from the outside — they change the interior of your business in measurable ways.

Glass transmission is affected by contamination. A layer of grime, dust film, or water spots on exterior glass can reduce visible light transmission by 10-20% under typical conditions. For retail, food service, or any space that benefits from natural light, this matters. Clean windows let more daylight in, reducing the hard, artificial-light look that dated retail spaces often have. Natural light improves the perceived quality of merchandise, the appearance of food in a display case, and the general atmosphere of a space.

The employee side of this equation is less often discussed but consistently observed by retail managers: employees who work in a bright, well-maintained space with clean windows report higher job satisfaction and show less fatigue than those in darker, neglected environments. This is partly about light itself and partly about the message maintenance sends to staff — that management cares about the environment they work in. For businesses in a regional market like Salmon Arm that compete for good employees, this is worth thinking about.

For offices and professional services — accountants, insurance brokers, healthcare practitioners — clean windows signal professionalism to clients waiting in reception areas. The cleanliness cue extends from the exterior to the interior, and the glass is the link between the two. A client sitting in a waiting room with grimy windows is already discounting their confidence in the practice before the appointment begins.
sections.body2: Natural light also matters for merchandising. Retail businesses that display products near windows — clothing, home goods, specialty food — benefit from the full color rendering that natural light provides. Dirty glass skews color perception and makes merchandise look duller than it is.

sections.h2: Property Value and Landlord-Tenant Considerations
sections.body:
For property owners in Salmon Arm, window cleanliness affects commercial property value in ways that extend beyond aesthetics. Commercial properties with consistently maintained exteriors — including clean windows — attract and retain better tenants. Prospective tenants tour properties and form impressions of ownership quality based on what they see. A building with clean, maintained windows signals that the owner takes pride in the asset; a building with years of grime on the glass signals neglect and raises questions about what maintenance items have also been deferred.

For current tenants, lease agreements often include provisions about exterior presentation standards. Window cleaning may be a landlord responsibility or a tenant responsibility depending on the lease structure — and this is worth clarifying, because ambiguity leads to windows that nobody cleans.

In Salmon Arm's commercial corridors — particularly along Highway 97B, the downtown core on Lakeshore Drive, and the growing commercial strip near the Askew's and Walmart area — buildings that maintain clean exteriors also benefit from increased visibility to passing traffic. A clean, clear window display is visible from a vehicle traveling at 50 km/h. A dirty window is not. For businesses that rely on drive-by visibility to generate walk-in traffic, this is a direct bottom-line factor.

h1: Commercial Window Cleaning Contracts in BC: What to Know Before You Sign
intro: A recurring commercial window cleaning contract is one of the simplest operational decisions a business can make — and one of the most underused. Regular scheduling, a locked rate, and zero calendar management from you. sections.h2: How Recurring Commercial Contracts Work
sections.body:
A commercial window cleaning contract is straightforward in concept: you agree to a regular schedule (weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly), a defined scope of work, and a fixed rate for a set term. In exchange, you get automatic scheduling with no call-in required, and we hold your rate for the contract period regardless of how our general pricing moves.

The scheduling side matters more than people realize. The most common reason commercial windows don't get cleaned as often as they should is administrative friction — someone has to remember to call, get a quote, coordinate a time, and approve the invoice every single visit. For a busy restaurant manager, retail supervisor, or property manager, this process gets deprioritized constantly. A contract removes it entirely. The cleaning happens. You get the invoice. Done.

Frequency options typically align with business type and traffic volume:
- **Weekly**: High-touch entry glass, restaurants with significant fingerprint and grease exposure, retail in high-traffic tourist zones during peak season
- **Bi-weekly**: General retail, service businesses, offices with public-facing windows, accommodations
- **Monthly**: Lower-traffic commercial, professional services, businesses on quieter streets, shoulder-season scheduling for tourism businesses

Some contracts use a seasonal model — monthly through fall and winter, bi-weekly or weekly during summer tourist season — which matches cleaning frequency to actual traffic and need. This is often the right structure for Shuswap businesses given how dramatically visitor volume changes between January and August.

sections.h2: What Should Be in a Good Commercial Cleaning Contract
sections.body:
Not all commercial cleaning agreements are written the same way, and the quality of the document reflects the quality of the service relationship. Before signing any commercial window cleaning contract in BC, know what to look for.

Ambiguity in scope leads to disputes later. If you have a specific window or section of glass you need included, name it in the contract.

**Frequency and scheduling windows**: The contract should state the agreed frequency and whether visits are on a fixed day or within a scheduling window (e.g., 'bi-weekly, Tuesday-Thursday between 7am-10am'). If your business has times that don't work for cleaning — Saturday lunch rush at a restaurant, Monday morning at a busy retail store — these should be noted explicitly.

**Rate and price escalation terms**: The base rate per visit should be stated. Understand whether the rate is fixed for the entire term or subject to annual adjustment, and if so, how much and with how much notice.

**Term and cancellation**: Most commercial contracts run 6 or 12 months with 30-day written notice for cancellation outside of cause. Understand the exit terms before you sign. A good service provider will have straightforward cancellation terms — not penalty-heavy lock-ins.

**Service standards and accountability**: What happens if a visit is missed? What's the process for flagging quality issues? A professional contract includes a service standard definition and a clear path for raising concerns.

meta_title: Clean Windows & Business First Impressions | Shuswap Guide
meta_desc: Psychology of storefront first impressions and how clean windows drive customer decisions. Research-backed guide for Shuswap and Salmon Arm business owners.
h1: First Impressions and Clean Windows: The Psychology Every Business Owner Needs to Know
intro: Customers decide how they feel about your business before they walk through the door — and the decision happens faster than you think. Research on retail customer psychology consistently finds that storefront presentation drives purchase intent in measurable ways, and window cleanliness sits at the center of that judgment. Here's what the research says, and what it means for Shuswap business owners specifically.

sections.h2: How Fast First Impressions Actually Form
sections.body:
The science of first impressions is humbling for anyone who believes rational quality assessment drives consumer choices. Research from Princeton University found that people form a confident judgment about a person's traits within 100 milliseconds of seeing their face — and subsequent exposure largely reinforces the initial judgment rather than correcting it. Applied to retail environments, similar mechanisms operate: customers form a 'feel' for a business within seconds of approaching it, and that feel influences whether they enter, how long they stay, and whether they buy.

Storefront windows are the primary visual interface between a passing potential customer and your business interior. They are literally what people see first, before signage, before the door, before any interior element. In a pedestrian retail environment like Salmon Arm's downtown Lakeshore strip, a potential customer walking by has approximately 2-4 seconds of sightline on your storefront before they're past it. In that window (no pun intended), they've already formed a preliminary verdict.

Dirty windows don't just fail to impress — they actively trigger a negative inference cascade. The psychological mechanism is straightforward: visible neglect in the most prominent exterior feature of a business signals negligence more broadly. If the owner doesn't clean the windows, what else are they not attending to? This inference happens below conscious awareness. The customer doesn't think 'their windows are dirty, therefore their food is probably of lower quality' — they just feel less inclined to go in, and they couldn't necessarily tell you why.

For businesses that rely on walk-in traffic — every restaurant, café, retail store, and tourism operator in the Shuswap — this unconscious filtering is costing real revenue. The customers who walked past without entering don't show up in any analytics. Their absence is invisible. But it's real.
bullets: First impressions form in under 100 milliseconds and are difficult to override
Dirty windows trigger negative quality inference before the customer consciously registers why
2-4 seconds of sightline in a pedestrian environment is the entire opportunity
Walk-in avoidance is invisible in analytics — absent customers leave no trace

sections.h2: Why Windows Are Different From Floors and Walls
sections.body:
Interior maintenance — clean floors, spotless tables, tidy shelving — matters enormously for customer experience once they're inside. But windows occupy a different category, and understanding this distinction helps business owners prioritize correctly.

Floors, walls, and shelving are noticed consciously when they're dirty, but customers don't necessarily see them before deciding to enter. Windows are pre-entry evaluation points. They're the one element of your business that customers assess before they've committed to engaging with you at all. This makes them categorically different from any interior cleanliness element.

Windows also have a transparency effect that no other surface shares. A window invites inspection of the interior — the seating environment, the merchandise display, the activity and energy inside. Clean glass maximizes this invitation. Dirty glass obscures it, replacing the interesting interior with a reflection of the street, a milky haze, or a pattern of water spots. The business interior — which may be beautifully designed and meticulously maintained — becomes invisible behind contaminated glass.

For retail, this matters most when merchandise is displayed near windows. Clothing, specialty food, gifts, or art displayed in a window become the primary advertisement for the interior of the store. Dirty glass between the viewer and the merchandise reduces color fidelity, contrast, and the overall appeal of the display. The business has invested in attractive merchandise and display, and dirty windows are silently discounting that investment from the outside.

For food service, window views into dining areas influence seating desirability and perceived quality. A clean window showing a warm, active dining room invites entry. A dirty window showing a vague interior impression does the opposite.
sections.body2: Interior cleanliness and exterior window cleanliness are both important — but they operate at different points in the customer decision process. Windows come first.

sections.h2: Business Types Where Window Cleanliness Has the Highest Impact
sections.body:
While clean windows benefit any business, the impact is highest in categories where visual presentation is most tied to customer decisions.

**Restaurants and cafes**: The restaurant decision — where to eat — is heavily influenced by visual assessment of the dining environment. Potential diners peer through windows before entering. A grimy window doesn't just look bad; it makes the interior look less appealing regardless of actual interior conditions. Restaurant exterior glass is fingerprinted by every customer who pauses to look at the menu in the window, meaning it degrades faster than most commercial glass.

**Retail and boutique shops**: Merchandise display windows are the core advertising surface for independent retailers. Every dollar spent on visual merchandising is discounted by dirty glass. In tourist markets like Salmon Arm and Sicamous, retail businesses compete for the attention of visitors who have no prior loyalty — the window is the entire pitch.

**Healthcare and professional services**: Dental offices, optometrists, chiropractors, and general practitioners deal with patient anxiety that is managed or exacerbated by environmental cues. A well-maintained exterior — clean windows, tidy entry — reduces anxiety at the access point and reinforces confidence in the practice's competency and attention to detail.

**Real estate and financial services**: These businesses sell trust in high-stakes decisions. Environmental signals that reinforce professionalism — including clean windows visible from the street — contribute to the overall trust impression. Offices in key street-facing locations in Salmon Arm benefit significantly from the professional signal that maintained exteriors provide.

**Accommodations**: Hotels, motels, and bed-and-breakfasts in the Shuswap serve guests who chose accommodation partly based on photography and reviews. When reality matches or exceeds expectation, reviews improve. Clean exterior windows are part of the 'kept' quality that guests notice and comment on positively.
bullets: Restaurants: window view into dining space influences entry decision directly
Retail/boutique: display windows are the primary advertising surface — dirty glass discounts them
Healthcare: window cleanliness manages patient anxiety and signals clinical competence
Real estate/financial: trust signals are environmental — clean exteriors reinforce credibility
Accommodations: maintained exterior drives better guest reviews and repeat bookings

sections.h2: Shuswap Tourism Businesses and the Presentation Competition
sections.body:
The Shuswap is a destination region with a well-documented seasonal tourism economy. For businesses in Salmon Arm, Sicamous, Sorrento, and along the Trans-Canada and Okanagan corridors, summer tourist traffic represents the majority of annual revenue for many operators.

The competitive context for presentation in a tourist market is different from a year-round local market. Local customers who visit weekly develop relationships and loyalty that override environmental cues. Tourists are single-transaction or first-time visitors making choices with no prior relationship and limited time. They are also comparing your business — consciously or not — to the competition they've seen at their last stop, and to the businesses immediately adjacent to yours on the same street.

In this context, presentation quality is a direct competitive variable. The restaurant with the cleanest, most inviting windows on a tourist street captures a disproportionate share of walk-in traffic from visitors who are simply choosing where to stop. They didn't find you on Google Maps first — they were walking past and made a snap judgment. That judgment is dominated by what they could see.

Shuswap tourism businesses that invest in presentation — including professional window cleaning on a scheduled cadence — are not just maintaining standards; they are actively competing for market share against adjacent businesses that don't. In the compressed peak season of June through August, capturing an incremental share of tourist traffic through better presentation can represent meaningful additional revenue.

The timing logic for Shuswap tourism businesses: be clean before the May long weekend, maintain through July and August, and do a fall clean before the end of the season in September. This is a three-clean annual schedule that keeps you competitive throughout the tourist calendar.

Adjacent businesses compete directly on presentation — tourists compare what they can see
May long weekend is the starting gun: be clean before it, not after
Three-clean schedule (spring, midsummer, fall) is the competitive minimum for tourism operators

sections.h2: Making the Investment Decision Easy
sections.body:
For many Shuswap small business owners, window cleaning feels like a cosmetic expense that gets deferred when cash flow is tight. The framing that changes the decision is cost-per-customer-acquisition — the most meaningful marketing metric for a business trying to grow.

A professional commercial window clean from Wagon Windows starts at $169. But the real number is likely higher. Monthly storefront cleaning on a high-traffic Salmon Arm location probably influences dozens of walk-in decisions over the course of the month, not just two. The cost-per-customer-influenced is well under a dollar. No other marketing spend available to a small business in the Shuswap comes close to that efficiency.

The business case for clean windows isn't a soft 'looks nice' argument. It's a hard acquisition-cost argument, expressed in the most concrete terms: you already have the advertising surface, you're already paying for the location, and the incremental cost to make that surface perform at its best is the smallest line item on your marketing budget.

Two additional $40 customers per month nearly covers the monthly clean cost alone
Real impact is likely many more influenced decisions, not just two

faqs.a: Yes. Customer perception research in retail environments consistently finds that storefront cleanliness — including windows — significantly affects purchase intent and likelihood of entry. Studies in the UK retail sector found that over 50% of shoppers said dirty windows deterred them from entering, and over 90% said window cleanliness affected their quality perception of the business. meta_title: Restaurant Window Cleaning BC: Schedule & Best Practices
meta_desc: Restaurant windows face unique challenges: grease film, fingerprints, and health inspection optics. A complete guide for BC restaurant owners on schedule and service.
h1: Restaurant Window Cleaning in BC: A Complete Guide for Food Service Operators
intro: Restaurant windows get dirtier faster than any other commercial glass — and they matter more to customers than almost any other business type. From kitchen exhaust grease to daily fingerprint accumulation on entry doors, food service operations face a window cleaning challenge that's specific enough to warrant its own framework. Here's what BC restaurant operators need to know.

sections.h2: Why Restaurant Windows Are a Unique Cleaning Challenge
sections.body:
Restaurant glass faces contamination sources that don't exist in any other commercial category. Understanding these specific challenges is the starting point for managing them effectively.

**Kitchen exhaust grease**: Commercial kitchen ventilation exhausts air through roof-mounted or wall-mounted hoods that often vent near windows or allow ambient grease-laden air to circulate outdoors. This grease deposits on exterior glass as a fine, invisible film that's chemically different from dust or mineral deposits. Grease film on glass creates a hazy, smeared appearance when it catches light, and it actively attracts and holds other particulate — pollen, dust, combustion byproducts from nearby vehicles. Standard window cleaning solutions don't cut grease well; a degreasing pre-treatment is needed for kitchen-adjacent windows.

**Entry glass fingerprints**: A busy restaurant's entry door glass is touched by every person who enters or exits. A lunch service with 50 covers generates at least 100 hand-contact events on the entry glass. Multiply that by dinner service and a full week, and entry glass accumulates an extraordinary fingerprint load. This is the most visible window in the building from a customer's perspective — it's the last thing they see before entering and the first thing they see on exit.

**Condensation and cooking steam**: Restaurants with visible kitchen areas, pass-through windows to kitchen stations, or steam-intensive cooking produce ambient moisture that deposits on interior glass surfaces. This combines with airborne grease to create a film on interior surfaces that's distinctly different from the exterior contamination challenge.
bullets: Grease film from kitchen exhaust: requires degreasing pre-treatment, not just standard window cleaning
Entry door fingerprints: 100+ contact events per day in a moderately busy restaurant
Condensation and steam: deposits on interior glass near kitchen areas
Patio window exposure: heavy use, finger contact, and outdoor particulate during service periods

sections.h2: How Often Restaurant Windows Should Be Cleaned
sections.body:
The right cleaning frequency for a restaurant depends on two variables: the contamination rate (how fast the glass gets dirty) and the presentation stakes (how much the glass condition matters to your specific customer experience).

For entry door glass, weekly cleaning is the realistic minimum for any restaurant with meaningful lunch and dinner service. Entry glass is the most-touched, most-noticed surface in the building from a customer perspective. A fingerprint-covered entry door communicates low hygiene standards before the customer has touched a menu. In peak season for Shuswap tourism restaurants — June through August — bi-weekly entry glass cleaning is often justified.

For dining room window glass and storefront glass facing the street, monthly professional cleaning is appropriate for most Salmon Arm restaurants. During summer tourist season, bi-weekly cleans maintain the standard better. The street-facing glass gets grease film and pollen buildup faster in summer due to vehicle traffic and seasonal particulate, and it's viewed by potential new customers who are making entry decisions.

Kitchen-adjacent exterior glass — windows near the exhaust hood or kitchen ventilation — may need more frequent cleaning depending on the grease output of your kitchen. If you're running a high-volume kitchen with a fryer, roaster, or grill, the windows within five meters of exhaust points will accumulate grease film measurably faster than other glass.

Patio windows and glass-panel railings used during summer service are a separate consideration. These get heavy outdoor use and fingerprint contact during patio service hours and may need mid-week touch-ups in addition to a regular professional visit schedule.
bullets: Entry door glass: weekly minimum, bi-weekly in peak season
Storefront and dining room windows: monthly standard, bi-weekly during tourist season
Kitchen-adjacent exterior glass: frequency depends on kitchen grease output
Patio glass and railings: assess separately based on summer service hours

sections.h2: Health Inspection Optics and Professional Standards
sections.body:
In British Columbia, food service establishments are subject to inspection by Fraser Health, Interior Health, or the relevant regional health authority. While window cleanliness is not a scored inspection category on its own, the overall impression of hygiene and maintenance that inspectors form — and that they note in qualitative assessments — is influenced by the full visual environment, including window condition.

More directly, health inspections create 'inspection pressure' events that make restaurant operators suddenly aware of presentation standards throughout the building. A restaurant that is generally well-maintained doesn't need to scramble before an inspection. A restaurant that defers maintenance — including window cleaning — finds itself doing emergency cleaning before inspection dates.

The practical recommendation for any BC restaurant operator is to maintain windows as part of your standard hygiene regime rather than treating them as a cosmetic item to address irregularly. Monthly professional cleaning, complemented by daily staff wipe-downs of entry glass, keeps the building in inspection-ready condition as a baseline.

For Shuswap restaurants that are reviewed online — Google, Yelp, TripAdvisor — window condition contributes to the review-worthy visual presentation of the establishment. Reviewers photograph restaurant exteriors and interiors. Dirty windows end up in photos. Those photos are associated with your Google Business profile. The reputational half-life of a bad photo outlasts the cleaning appointment it represents by months or years.
sections.body2: Online review photography is permanent. A dirty exterior in a reviewer's photo stays associated with your business listing long after the window has been cleaned. Maintain the standard, don't just catch up before inspections.

sections.h2: What a Proper Restaurant Window Service Includes
sections.body:
A professional restaurant window cleaning service should account for the specific contamination profile of a food service operation, not just apply the same approach used on an office or retail space.

The scope of a proper restaurant service includes:

**Exterior street-facing glass**: Full exterior clean including storefront windows, any display windows, and glazed panels adjacent to the entry. Grease film pre-treatment applied to kitchen-adjacent windows before the standard clean.

**Entry door glass (both faces)**: Interior and exterior of all entry and exit doors. Fingerprint removal is the primary task here. This is often the highest-impact single element of a restaurant window clean and should never be skipped.

**Interior dining room glass**: Windows and glazed partitions visible to diners. Condensation films and interior cooking deposit need to be addressed, not just the exterior surfaces.

**Frames and sills**: Window frames accumulate grime that migrates to glass during the next rain event. A clean that doesn't address frames leaves the source of re-contamination in place.

**Patio glass (seasonal)**: For restaurants with outdoor seating areas bounded by glass panels, seasonal patio glass cleaning as part of a service visit maintains the patio environment.

Wagon Windows serves restaurants in Salmon Arm and throughout the Shuswap. We understand the specific contamination challenges of food service environments and schedule around your service hours — early morning before opening or in the early afternoon between lunch and dinner service — to minimize operational disruption.

Entry door glass: both faces, full fingerprint removal
Interior dining room glass: condensation and cooking deposit removal
Frames and sills: clean the source, not just the surface
Patio glass: seasonal cleaning integrated into regular service schedule

faqs.q: How do you remove grease film from restaurant exterior windows?
faqs.a: Grease film requires a degreasing pre-treatment before standard window cleaning solution is applied. We use a diluted degreaser on affected panels, allow dwell time, then scrub with a soft sleeve before squeegee finishing. Standard window cleaning solution alone doesn't cut grease effectively and leaves a smeared result if the pre-treatment step is skipped.

faqs.a: Window cleanliness is not a scored inspection item on BC health authority inspection forms. However, the overall hygiene impression matters to inspectors and is reflected in qualitative notes. More importantly, exterior restaurant photos taken by customers and reviewers end up associated with your online business listings — and dirty window photos have a long online lifespan.

faqs.a: Yes, and most of our restaurant clients are on recurring contracts — weekly for entry glass, monthly for full exterior, or a combined schedule that makes sense for their operation. meta_title: Why Are Windows Streaky After Cleaning? Causes & Fixes
meta_desc: Streaky windows after cleaning come from specific, diagnosable causes. Here's how to identify which one you have and how to fix it — including what to do if a pro left streaks.
h1: Why Are My Windows Streaky After Cleaning? Root Causes and Real Fixes
intro: Finishing a window clean and seeing streaks is one of the most frustrating outcomes in household maintenance. The problem feels simple but the cause isn't always obvious — and applying the wrong fix to the wrong cause makes it worse. Here's how to diagnose which streak problem you actually have, and how to resolve each one cleanly.

sections.h2: The Five Root Causes of Streaky Windows
sections.body:
Before reaching for the spray bottle again, it's worth understanding that streaks come from fundamentally different sources, each with a different solution. Treating them all the same way — spray more, wipe harder — is why the streak problem persists for so many people.

**Cause 1: Wrong solution concentration.** Window cleaning solutions need to be diluted correctly to work properly. Too concentrated and the solution leaves a soapy film when it dries; too dilute and it doesn't cut the oils and biofilm on the glass surface, leaving residue behind. Most DIY cleaners either use undiluted dish soap (too concentrated, too much surfactant) or overly diluted mixtures. Professionals use purpose-formulated window cleaning solution at the correct dilution ratio for the temperature and glass condition.

**Cause 2: Dirty squeegee rubber.** A squeegee rubber blade accumulates grit, dried cleaning solution, and glass-surface oils with every pass. On a dirty rubber, the contamination is redistributed across the glass rather than cleared. This is the single most common cause of streaks in DIY cleaning. The rubber should be wiped with a clean damp cloth between passes, and replaced when it shows nicks, hardening, or permanent deformation along the edge.

**Cause 3: Detailing cloth that's not dry enough.** The 'detail' step — edging and corner work with a microfiber cloth — is where most streaks are introduced. If the cloth is damp from a previous use, or was left in a wet state, it redistributes moisture rather than absorbing it. Detailing cloths should be dry and clean. In professional cleaning, multiple cloths are staged so there's always a fresh dry one available.

**Cause 4: Technique timing.** In warm weather or direct sun, solution dries faster than the squeegee can remove it. When dried solution is pushed by the squeegee, it streaks. The fix is to work in the shade when possible, work faster, or add a small amount of solution to slow the drying time. In cold weather, the reverse problem occurs — solution dries too slowly and needs extra detailing work.

**Cause 5: Mineral-laden water.** Hard water contains dissolved calcium and magnesium. When water dries on glass, these minerals are left behind as white, chalky deposits. If you're mixing cleaning solution with tap water in a hard-water area — like much of the Shuswap region — the minerals in your mixing water can streak even with perfect technique.
bullets: Wrong solution concentration: too soapy = film; too dilute = oily residue
Dirty squeegee rubber: redistributes contamination instead of removing it
Damp detailing cloth: smears moisture instead of absorbing it
Timing: direct sun or heat dries solution before squeegee completes the pass
Hard water: dissolved minerals left on glass when tap water is used undiltered

sections.h2: How to Diagnose Which Problem You Have
sections.body:
Streaks look similar to the naked eye, but the pattern tells you the source if you know what to look for.

**Soap film streaks** look like a uniform milky haze across the glass, not individual lines. They don't have a directional pattern. They're worse in areas where more solution was applied. When you breathe on the glass, the fogging is uneven — soapy areas fog differently than clean areas. Fix: reduce solution concentration significantly. Start with 1-2 drops of dish soap in a 4-litre bucket.

**Dirty rubber streaks** appear as parallel lines following the path of the squeegee stroke. The pattern is directional and consistent with the squeegee direction. The lines repeat at roughly equal intervals, matching the length of the rubber blade. Fix: wipe the rubber edge thoroughly, test on a corner pane, and if the blade is damaged or hardened, replace it.

**Detailing cloth streaks** appear in corners, along the edge where rubber meets frame, and at the bottom of the pane where detail work is done. They're often curved or irregular — the shape follows the cloth wiping motion rather than the squeegee direction. Fix: switch to a dry microfiber cloth and work from the corner outward with light pressure.

**Hard water streaks** are most visible in raking sunlight at an angle. They appear as white or grayish haze that doesn't wipe off with a dry cloth. They're most concentrated near the bottom of the pane, where rinse water flows and dries. Fix: use distilled water for mixing, or apply a diluted white vinegar solution (1:1 with water) to dissolve mineral deposits before the main clean.
sections.body2: If you've corrected technique, solution, and tools and still have streaks, read section four — the problem may not be with the cleaning at all.

sections.h2: Fixing Each Streak Type: The Practical Steps
sections.body:
Once you've diagnosed the cause, the fix is straightforward.

**For soap film**: Completely rinse the glass with clean water to remove existing residue. Mix fresh solution at a much lower concentration — one small drop of dish soap per litre of water, or use a commercial window cleaning concentrate at the manufacturer's dilution. Re-clean from scratch.

**For dirty rubber streaks**: Buy a replacement squeegee rubber. They're inexpensive and this is the single best investment in DIY window cleaning quality. Install it correctly — blade seated evenly, no wobble — and wipe it between passes with a clean damp cloth. Hold the squeegee at a consistent angle (about 30 degrees) throughout each stroke.

**For detailing cloth streaks**: Use only dry, lint-free microfiber cloths. Wring them out fully if they've been used previously, then hang to dry before reuse. For corner detail work, fold the cloth to a fresh quadrant for each corner. Work from the top corner down with light pressure.

**For hard water mineral streaks**: Mix a 1:1 white vinegar and water solution. Apply to the affected glass with a cloth, allow one minute of dwell time, then wipe off. The acid in the vinegar dissolves calcium carbonate deposits that water alone can't remove. Follow with a standard window clean to remove the vinegar residue. For severe mineral buildup, a commercial calcium remover product may be needed before this step.
bullets: Soap film: rinse completely, remake solution at much lower concentration
Dirty rubber: replace the rubber blade, wipe between passes
Cloth streaks: always use dry lint-free microfiber, fresh quadrant per corner
Hard water: white vinegar 1:1 pre-treatment dissolves calcium deposits

sections.h2: When the Glass Is the Problem, Not the Cleaning
sections.body:
If you've corrected all five variables and still have streaks or haze, the glass itself may be the issue. This is more common than most homeowners realize, and it's important to identify it correctly so you don't waste time trying to clean away something that isn't a cleaning problem.

**Old or weathered glass**: Glass surfaces develop micro-abrasions over many years of weathering, cleaning, and UV exposure. These micro-abrasions scatter light at angles that create a hazy appearance. This is most visible on south-facing and west-facing windows with high UV exposure. There's no cleaning solution for micro-abrasion; it's a glass surface condition that can only be addressed by glass polishing compounds (specialized products) or ultimately window replacement.

**Etched glass surface**: Repeated exposure to hard water deposits that aren't fully removed causes glass surface etching over time. Unlike mineral deposits that sit on the surface, etching is actual surface damage — the calcium has chemically reacted with the silica in the glass. Etched glass has a permanently hazy appearance that doesn't improve with cleaning. Mild etching can sometimes be improved with glass polishing compounds; severe etching typically requires replacement.

**Failed window seal (fogging between panes)**: If the haze appears to be inside the glass — specifically between the two panes of a double-pane window — this is a failed insulated glass unit seal, not a cleaning issue. No amount of cleaning addresses this. See our guide on foggy windows for the full explanation and remediation path.
bullets: Old/weathered glass: micro-abrasions create permanent haze not removable by cleaning
Etched glass: calcium has chemically reacted with glass surface — cleaning won't help
Failed window seal: haze between panes is a structural repair issue, not a cleaning issue

faqs.q: A professional cleaner left streaks on my windows. What should I do?
faqs.a: Contact the company immediately and describe the issue specifically — where the streaks are, what they look like, when you noticed them. Professional window cleaners should stand behind their work with a satisfaction guarantee. A reputable company will return to address streaks at no charge. If the company is unresponsive or dismissive, that's a service quality red flag worth noting in a review.

faqs.q: Why do my windows look worse after cleaning than before?
faqs.a: The most common cause is that cleaning with incorrect solution or technique has redistributed existing contamination rather than removing it. Another possibility is that the glass has existing mineral deposits or haze that were hidden by surface grime — once the surface layer is removed, the underlying condition becomes visible. Starting with a white vinegar pre-treatment, rinsing thoroughly, and re-cleaning with fresh solution usually resolves the first problem.

faqs.q: Does the brand of window cleaner matter for avoiding streaks?
faqs.a: More than the brand, the concentration matters. Commercial products like Windex or Method work well when applied correctly — but 'correctly' usually means less product than most people use. Excess cleaner is the primary cause of streaky DIY cleans. Professional window cleaners use very dilute purpose-formulated solutions, not consumer spray products applied heavily.

faqs.q: Is there a way to make windows stay streak-free longer?
faqs.a: Yes — hydrophobic glass treatments (Rain-X and similar products) create a water-shedding surface that causes water to bead and run off rather than spreading and drying. Applied after a thorough clean, these products reduce water spotting and slow re-dirtying. They need re-application every few months but significantly extend the clean appearance between professional visits.

cta_text: Tired of streaky DIY results? Let Wagon Windows handle it properly. Call David at (403) 837-5092 for a streak-free clean in Salmon Arm and the Shuswap.

meta_title: Windows Foggy Inside: Causes, Fixes & When to Call Who
meta_desc: Foggy windows between the panes can't be cleaned away — it's a failed seal. Learn the causes, how to identify it, what repairs cost, and who to call in the Shuswap.
h1: Windows Foggy Inside: Why It Happens and What You Can Actually Do About It
intro: If you've noticed a hazy or foggy appearance on your windows that doesn't wipe away from either the inside or outside surface, you're dealing with a failed window seal — and calling a window cleaner won't help. This is one of the most common window problems in BC homes and one of the most misunderstood. Here's exactly what's happening, what your options are, and who to call.

sections.h2: What's Actually Causing the Fog Between the Panes
sections.body:
Modern double-pane and triple-pane windows are built as insulated glass units (IGUs). The IGU consists of two (or three) glass panes separated by a spacer bar, with the space between them sealed airtight and filled with insulating gas — typically argon or krypton. This sealed gas space is what gives modern windows their thermal performance. It's why a double-pane window in a Salmon Arm home dramatically outperforms a single-pane window on cold Shuswap winters.

The fogging you see between the panes is caused by this seal failing. When the perimeter seal degrades — due to age, UV exposure, water infiltration at the frame, or thermal cycling stress — outside air (and moisture) begins to infiltrate the space between the panes. Moisture-laden air entering the sealed unit condenses on the cooler inner glass surfaces and appears as fog, haze, or in advanced cases as visible water droplets between the panes.

The seal failure process is progressive. In early stages, fogging appears only on cold mornings and clears as the glass warms — because the moisture evaporates back out when temperatures equalize. As the seal degrades further, the fogging becomes more persistent. Eventually, mineral deposits from the condensed water leave a permanent haze on the inner glass surfaces that doesn't clear even in warm weather. At this stage, the glass appearance is permanently impaired regardless of exterior temperature.

Argon gas escaping from a failed seal contributes to the problem beyond aesthetics. Argon provides roughly 25-30% better thermal insulation than air. A compromised IGU with escaped argon performs like a much older window, with higher heat loss in winter and heat gain in summer. The foggy appearance is often the visible symptom of a larger energy efficiency problem.
bullets: IGU seal failure allows outside air and moisture into the sealed space between panes
Moisture condenses on cooler inner glass surfaces, creating visible fog or haze
Early-stage failure: fog clears when glass warms; advanced failure: permanent haze
Argon gas escapes when seal fails, reducing window thermal performance by 25-30%

sections.h2: Why Cleaning Can't Fix This Problem
sections.body:
This is the critical point that homeowners most often misunderstand: the fog is not on the glass surface — it is between the panes, on the inner faces of the glass that cannot be accessed without disassembling the window unit.

Cleaning the exterior glass surface, cleaning the interior glass surface, or applying any cleaning solution to the accessible faces of the glass has zero effect on fog that is physically located in the sealed space between the panes. Scrubbing harder, using stronger solutions, or hiring a professional cleaner will not change what you're seeing. The condensation or mineral deposit is on the interior faces of the glass, sealed inside the unit.

This is not something a window cleaner — no matter how experienced — can address through cleaning services. It's a different problem entirely, requiring either IGU replacement or full window replacement. Understanding this distinction saves homeowners money and frustration: calling a window cleaner for foggy double-pane windows results in paying for a service that can't help, with the fog still present afterward.

The only cleaning-adjacent service that occasionally has marginal benefit is the use of specialized defogging kits, which involve drilling small holes in the spacer bar and injecting a desiccant compound. This is sometimes offered by window restoration companies and produces mixed results — the haze may improve temporarily, but the underlying seal failure continues, and the long-term outcome is typically continued degradation. Most window professionals do not recommend defogging kits as a permanent solution.
sections.body2: If you've hired window cleaners and the fog persists, that's not a sign the cleaners did poor work. The fog is inaccessible to cleaning. This conversation should have been had at the quote stage — if a window cleaner doesn't identify failed seals during assessment, that's a service quality gap worth noting.

sections.h2: How to Tell a Failed Seal from Surface Condensation
sections.body:
Not every foggy-looking window has a failed seal. Surface condensation — moisture that forms on the accessible faces of the glass — is a different, benign phenomenon that doesn't require repair.

Surface condensation on the interior glass face occurs when warm, humid interior air contacts cold glass in winter. It appears as droplets or a misty film on the room-facing surface of the glass — the surface you can touch. It wipes away easily with a cloth and is gone by midday as the room warms. This is not a defect; it's normal physics. It can be reduced by increasing ventilation, using a dehumidifier, or adding window insulation film to raise the glass surface temperature.

Surface condensation on the exterior glass face happens on cool mornings when warm, humid outdoor air contacts glass that has been cooled overnight. This wipes away from outside and clears as the sun warms the glass. Again, not a defect.

A failed seal shows characteristics that distinguish it clearly: the fog or haze appears to be inside the glass, not on a touchable surface. Wiping the interior and exterior surfaces doesn't affect it. It may change appearance with temperature but doesn't wipe away. In advanced cases, there are visible mineral deposit patterns — white cloudy areas or 'tide marks' — within the glass unit that remain even in warm, dry weather.

The simplest test: on a dry indoor day, fog the glass with your breath and immediately check if the haze you see wipes away with your hand. If it does, it's surface condensation. If the haze is behind where your breath condensed, and doesn't wipe, it's inside the unit.
bullets: Interior surface condensation: touchable, wipes away easily, a normal physics phenomenon
Exterior surface condensation: occurs on cool mornings, clears as temperature rises
Failed seal fog: appears inside the glass, does not wipe away from either surface
Breath test: fog your breath on the glass and check whether the haze is on or inside the surface

sections.h2: IGU Replacement vs Full Window Replacement: The Cost Calculation
sections.body:
If you've confirmed a failed seal, the decision comes down to whether to replace the glass unit alone (IGU replacement) or the entire window assembly (frame, hardware, and glass).

**IGU replacement** is the lower-cost option when the window frame is in good condition. A glazier removes the failed glass unit from the existing frame and installs a new IGU. In BC, IGU replacement for a standard residential window typically costs $150-$400 per unit depending on size, glass type, and whether upgrades are specified. The existing frame, hardware, and installation are retained.

IGU replacement makes sense when: the frame is structurally sound, the seals around the frame are intact, the hardware operates correctly, and the window is less than 20-25 years old. It's a cost-effective repair that restores the window's appearance and thermal performance.

**Full window replacement** is warranted when: the frame is rotting or damaged, the window is approaching end of life (typically 20-30 years for a well-maintained unit), or when multiple IGUs in the same window assembly are failing simultaneously (suggesting a frame or installation issue rather than an isolated glass defect).

In Salmon Arm and the Shuswap, the freeze-thaw climate is relatively hard on window seals compared to warmer BC regions. Homes built in the 1990s with original windows may be approaching the point where multiple IGUs are starting to fail — at which point full window replacement on a room-by-room basis makes more economic sense than repairing individual units.

For specific quotes on IGU replacement in the Salmon Arm area, contact a local glazier or window company. This is not a service Wagon Windows provides — our expertise is in window cleaning and exterior maintenance, not glass repair. We're happy to confirm whether what you're seeing is a cleaning issue or a failed seal when we visit.
bullets: IGU replacement: $150-400 per unit in BC, viable when frame is sound
Full replacement: warranted when frame is damaged, window is aging, or multiple IGUs failing
Salmon Arm's freeze-thaw climate is harder on window seals than warmer BC regions
1990s-era homes may be at the point where multiple seals are failing simultaneously

faqs.q: Can a window cleaner fix foggy windows between panes?
faqs.a: No. The fog is inside the sealed glass unit, on the inner faces of the glass that cannot be accessed through cleaning. No cleaning technique, solution, or tool addresses this. A window cleaner can clean the accessible exterior and interior surfaces, but the fog between panes requires IGU replacement by a glazier — not a cleaning service.

faqs.q: My window is foggy only on cold mornings. Does it have a failed seal?
faqs.a: Not necessarily. Fog that appears only on cold mornings and clears completely by midday is often normal surface condensation — not a failed seal. The distinguishing feature is whether the fog wipes away from the accessible glass surface. If it wipes away from the interior or exterior surface, it's surface condensation. If it appears inside the glass and doesn't wipe away from either surface, it's a failed seal.

faqs.q: Is it worth repairing a foggy double-pane window or should I replace the whole thing?
faqs.a: If the window frame is in good condition and only one or two glass units have failed, IGU replacement is typically the better value. If you have an older home with multiple failing IGUs, aging frames, and hardware that's increasingly difficult to operate, full replacement may be more cost-effective over a 10-year horizon. Get quotes from a glazier for both options before deciding.

faqs.q: What's the difference between a window cleaner and a glazier?
faqs.a: A window cleaner cleans accessible glass surfaces — removing dirt, mineral deposits, hard water stains, and grime from the exterior and interior faces of your windows. A glazier works with the glass structure itself — cutting, installing, and replacing glass units. Failed seals, cracked glass, and IGU replacement are glazier work. Dirty glass surfaces are window cleaner work. The two services are complementary, not interchangeable.

cta_text: Not sure if your windows need cleaning or replacing? Wagon Windows will give you an honest assessment. Call David at (403) 837-5092.
